evidence_quotes.quote: they are often hidden and even when TOS are written or linked somewhere, they are really difficult to read, with misleading phrases
they do not make clear that the "buy" button means "rent"
the TOS that you accepted at the time of your purchase is no longer the one that is in effect today
TOS (terms of service) are inside the installer of the product or after the purchasing screen, they are often hidden

paragraphs.text: Please, I would like to ask you to include on it some ways to avoiding losing buyed products such as movies, books, anime and games. In the past (for example when Funimation and Crunchyroll merged together) legally acquired media vanished in a blink of an eye, just because the company that sold their digital version choose to remove them despite the company was still running its business; sometimes it happened after two big competitors merged into one creating a monopoly and unilaterally erasing buyed products from users libraries overnight, other times it happened just because the higher ups of a company felt like the existence of an old product may decrease the sales of a new game, a new app, anime or other. Sometimes companies take your money and just run away, they promise you a online-only product and after you pay them, they close their server leaving you with nothing and without any legal way to consume the product that you buyed. This kind of behavior is present in many fields of online transactions. I'm not asking for forcing the seller(s) of these online-only products to keep paying to maintain servers online forever, but when they sold you something they should let you "experience" your purchased product, they should not shut down a newly buyed product without a reasonable prior notice, they should as well provide alternative ways to let the consumer keep enjoying his/her buyed product by letting the consumer download a off-line version of whatever he/she payed for (a movie, anime, game, a piece of art or whatever item it was), or provide ways to keep using said product online from other suppliers, enabling the possibility to run private "free" servers with the clause that the private server cannot be hosted for commercial purpose (selling items to players without the explicit consent of the copyright owner(s) will be not allowed), or other ways... What matters it's that if you buy something, you can somehow have access to it. It's as well needed to make a clear distinction between real purchasing and "rented" products, since often companies sell something, but they do not make clear that the "buy" button means "rent" and sometimes TOS (terms of service) are inside the installer of the product or after the purchasing screen, they are often hidden and even when TOS are written or linked somewhere, they are really difficult to read, with misleading phrases, they are always subjected to changes (so the TOS that you accepted at the time of your purchase is no longer the one that is in effect today) and so on. On top of all these mentioned problems, now credit card companies are unilaterally selecting what contents/products are fair to be consumed and purchased and which ones are not, despite we already have laws for telling which product can be sold and buyed and which cannot. We as well already have ways to tell if a kid can or can't buy something, since legal platforms already apply filters to prevent minors from unintentionally see or buy adult and sensitive contents/products; in addition to that a minor simply can't open a bank account and get a credit card, so if a minor manage to avoid existing blocks and warning it's because an adult let the minor do it, providing all the tools needed to access it (internet connection, a device with a browser/app to use internet and get the product by faking to be an adult, the money and the online payment system tied to an adult citizen, the usually mandatory verify code needed to finalize online purchasing which is frequently tied to a phone number or a e-mail address). It's true that a minor may fake to be an adult, but a minor still need adults money and adult games does not allow minors to try them, not even with parents consent. Just not giving minors access to credit cards will solve that kind of problem, without changing anything else. It's like when kids drink alcohol, despite it's against the laws, just because the parents let them do it. Thanks for your attention.
